seoTitle: Werrington Downs vs West Albury — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Werrington Downs and West Albury. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Werrington Downs vs West Albury: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Werrington Downs and West Albury.
heading: Werrington Downs vs West Albury — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Werrington Downs, New South Wales and West Albury, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Albury has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Werrington Downs (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Werrington Downs or West Albury safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Werrington Downs has a flood risk score of 21/100, while West Albury scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Werrington Downs scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. West Albury scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Werrington Downs or West Albury?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Albury has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
